title: coronilla juncea subsp. pomelii
intro: Coronilla juncea subsp. pomelii is a subspecies of the annual or biennial plant Coronilla juncea, also known as the sticklewort or crown vetch, which belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Coronilla juncea subsp. pomelii include its ability to soothe and calm the respiratory system, making it a popular herbal remedy for conditions like asthma and tuberculosis. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, often in the form of tea, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
